reasoning: The recent March 2025 election victory by "independence gradualists" strongly indicates the newly formed government will not rush a referendum on independence. Gradualist factions in Greenland (such as Inuit Ataqatigiit or Siumut) prioritize building economic independence over an immediate formal declaration or referendum. The forecast window is incredibly short, spanning only about 60 days from the May 2, 2025 knowledge cutoff until the July 1, 2025 deadline. Given the explicitly gradualist mandate of the new government, the time required to negotiate government priorities, and the short timeline for such a major constitutional procedure, an announcement of an independence referendum before July 1, 2025, is highly improbable.
